overview: Mono White Aggro is a concise, aggressive Pauper deck focused on cheap creatures with useful ETBs and flexible disruptive effects. It aims to apply early pressure while drawing cards through one-drops and token generators. The deck leverages versatile removal and board protection to navigate the current metagame and secure quick victories.
win_conditions: Deploy cheap creatures with ETBs to apply early pressure
Generate virtual card advantage and token armies using Raffine’s Informant with Battle Screech or Prismatic Strands
Push lethal attacks protected by Guardians’ Pledge
Use flexible removal like Thraben Charm to clear paths and control the board

keep_criteria: Hands with multiple cheap one-drops like Novice Inspector or Lunarch Veteran
Early interaction or removal to handle aggressive starts
A mix of creatures and card draw to maintain pressure
Keep any hand with a land and a 1-drop (Novice Inspector) or a 2-drop (Kor Skyfisher).
Keep hands with Thraben Charm if paired with a creature or land.
mulligan_criteria: Lack of early pressure or fast clock
Hands heavy on lands or high-cost cards without early plays
No answers to expected aggressive threats
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only 3+ mana costs, or hands with multiple charms but no threats.

examples.reason: Provides early pressure, card draw, and interaction on curve.
Lacks early creatures or pressure to establish a fast clock.

early_game: Play cheap one-drops like Novice Inspector and Lunarch Veteran to generate clues and apply pressure
Use Lunarch Veteran for efficient chump blocks to gain extra turns
Hold up mana for flexible removal like Thraben Charm
Turn 2: Play a land, cast Novice Inspector (or Kor Skyfisher if you have 2W).
If you have a charm, use it to clear a potential blocker or protect your attacker.
sequence fetch/shock lands to ensure no untapped lands on turn 1/2.
mid_game: Cast Battle Screech or Prismatic Strands alongside Raffine’s Informant to create token armies and draw cards
Use Kor Skyfisher to reuse ETBs for additional value
Protect key creatures and push damage with Guardians’ Pledge
Use Thraben Charm's protection mode to save key threats from spot removal.
Keep a card in hand for interaction.
Graveyard is generally irrelevant for mono-white aggro, but watch for opponent's recursion.
Commit when you have card advantage or when the opponent has no removal.
Hold back if they have sweepers or spot removal unanswered.
late_game: Deploy Eagles of the North and Idyllic Grange for board presence and +1/+1 counters
Sideboard in targeted hate cards like Dust to Dust, Destroy Evil, or Coalition Honor Guard based on matchup
Maintain board control with Thraben Charm and token generation to close out games
Kor Skyfisher and other flyers are primary finishers.
Use Thraben Charm's damage mode to close out games.
Equipment or direct damage can finish.
When you have 3+ threats on board, opponent has no removal/sweeper, and you can attack for lethal or force a trade that leaves you ahead.

key_cards.name: Novice Inspector
key_cards.notes: One-drop that generates Clues when it enters the battlefield, enabling early card advantage.

key_cards.name: Lunarch Veteran
key_cards.notes: Main weapon against Kuldotha Red and Aggro; offers 2-for-1 potential against removal and efficient chump blocks.

key_cards.name: Thraben Charm
key_cards.notes: Provides almost unconditional removal, counters enchantments like Sadistic Glee, and answers graveyards/reanimator.

key_cards.name: Raffine’s Informant
key_cards.notes: Cheap drop that draws cards and synergizes with Battle Screech/Prismatic Strands for virtual card advantage.

key_cards.name: Battle Screech
key_cards.notes: Creates 1/1 tokens, interacts with Informant, and can push lethal when protected by Guardians’ Pledge.

key_cards.name: Guardians’ Pledge
key_cards.notes: Functions like Rally the Peasants to protect from sweepers or enable lethal attacks with tokens.

key_cards.name: Suture Priest
key_cards.notes: Locks Basking Broodscale and Sadistic Glee; flexible slot that may become a four-of if those threats grow.

key_cards.name: Coalition Honor Guard
key_cards.notes: Evades Drown in Sorrow and forces unfavorable trades against Kuldotha Red, though vulnerable to Snuff Out.

sequencing_tips: Pair Battle Screech or Prismatic Strands with Raffine’s Informant to maximize token generation and card draw
Use Lunarch Veteran for chump blocks to gain tempo and extra turns against aggressive decks
Hold up mana for Thraben Charm to handle removal, enchantments, or graveyard strategies flexibly
Avoid overextending with Battle Screech against decks that run End the Festivities
Use Guardians’ Pledge proactively to protect against sweepers like Drown in Sorrow or Breath Weapon
Common mistake: overcommitting to the board without assessing sweepers.
Unique interaction: Thraben Charm's modes can be split across targets if you have multiple targets, but typically used on one.
Watch out for opponent's 'bounce' or 'tuck' effects.
Always keep a land in play to ensure you can cast your threats on curve.

matchups.advice: Maindeck Lunarch Veteran and sideboard Coalition Honor Guard, Holy Light, and Prismatic Strands. Force 2-for-1 trades or make them waste Galvanic Blast.
Sideboard in Standard Bearer and Revoke Existence to handle enchantments and key threats.
Sideboard in Dust to Dust and Revoke Existence to shut down artifact synergies.
Sideboard in Suture Priest, Standard Bearer, and Coalition Honor Guard. Maintain a fast clock to avoid being stalled.
Sideboard in Destroy Evil for creature removal and board control.
Sideboard in Destroy Evil and Prismatic Strands to handle large creatures and buy time.
Use Thraben Charm's protection mode on key spells. Attack with multiple threats to force multiple counters. Don't overcommit to a single expensive threat.
Apply maximum pressure early. Don't let them stabilize. Use removal on their mana dorks or early threats.
Trade efficiently. Use Thraben Charm to protect your blockers or remove their key attackers. Don't overextend into their combat tricks.
Pressure relentlessly. Use card draw to out-value them. Force them to use removal on low-value threats to exhaust their hand.
Value trades. Use Thraben Charm to protect your threats or remove theirs. Prioritize card advantage. Flying threats win combat.

common_threats: Cheap sweepers like Drown in Sorrow and Breath Weapon
Excessive removal such as Galvanic Blast and Snuff Out
Game plans hard to interact with like Familiars, Turbo Fog, and Gardens
Watch for sweepers (Wrath, etc.), spot removal (Path, Swords), and combo win conditions.
Note when they tap out for a counter or removal.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Mono White Aggro (or White Weenie) is an extremely concise deck in its game plan and follows the same old line: cheap creatures with useful ETBs and/or disruptive effects against the current Metagame.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: cheap creatures with useful ETBs and/or disruptive effects
good interactions with Raffine’s Informant to generate virtual card advantage
use it both for a lethal attack with Battle Screech tokens and to protect our creatures from sweepers

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: good interactions with Raffine’s Informant to generate virtual card advantage
offering a 2-for-1 against removal and/or even an efficient chump block exchange to gain a few extra turns
being such a flexible piece that it deserves four slots in the maindeck
it's not the best card for the matchup because it encourages overextending yourself with Battle Screech - which can be answered with End the Festivities
protect our creatures from sweepers like Drown in Sorrow or Breath Weapon

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: suffers from the same old problems: cheap sweepers, excessive removal and game plans that it has a hard time interacting with
hate against powerful enchantments like Sadistic Glee
locks Basking Broodscale and Sadistic Glee
you can't beat Familiars, Turbo Fog or Gardens even if you dedicate too many slots against them
